findings.description: Cover page collage image is split into multiple separate images in HTML instead of appearing as a single cohesive composite image
findings.evidence: PDF shows a bordered composite image with map, photos of lake, vegetation, and survey activities. HTML shows these as separate fragmented images without the unified border/frame.

findings.description: NC State University logo appears as a separate element below the collage in HTML rather than at the bottom of the page as in PDF
findings.evidence: PDF has the logo centered at bottom of page with 'Aquatic Plant Management Program' text below it. HTML has the logo appearing between the image fragments and the text.

findings.description: Table 1 styling differs - HTML uses a cleaner table format but the data structure and content are preserved
findings.evidence: PDF table has gray header row and alternating formatting. HTML table has a different visual style but same column structure and data.

findings.description: Figure 1 bar graph is not rendered as an image in HTML - it appears as raw text/data values instead of a visual chart
findings.evidence: PDF shows a bar graph titled 'Total Number of Survey Sites - By Year' with bars and values. HTML renders it as text: '5775 6564 5378 5714 6178 6503 5006 6041 5368 4938 5954 6311'

findings.description: Figure 2 bar graph is not rendered as an image in HTML - it appears as text labels instead of a visual chart
findings.evidence: PDF shows a colored bar graph of 'Lake Gaston Aquatic Plant Community - 2023'. HTML shows only text labels: 'Emergent Submergent Floating Algae' and species names as plain text.

findings.description: Figure 7 (Lake Gaston Hydrilla chart) is not rendered as an image in HTML - appears as raw text/numbers
findings.evidence: PDF shows a dual-axis bar/line chart of hydrilla acreage and rake tosses. HTML renders only axis labels and numbers as text.

findings.description: Figure 9 (Lake Gaston Lyngbya chart) is not rendered as an image in HTML - appears as raw text/numbers
findings.evidence: PDF shows a dual-axis bar/line chart of lyngbya acreage and rake tosses. HTML renders only axis labels and numbers as text.

findings.description: Figure 11 (Lake Gaston Eel Grass chart) is not rendered as an image in HTML - appears as raw text/numbers
findings.evidence: PDF shows a dual-axis bar/line chart of eel grass acreage and survey sites. HTML renders only axis labels and numbers as text.

findings.description: The HTML uses nested <main> tags, which is structurally invalid HTML. The document has two <main> elements, one inside the other.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '<main>' immediately inside another '<main>' element.

findings.description: The 'Conclusion and Management Implications' section is rendered as h2 while its continuation 'Conclusion and Management Implications (con't)' is rendered as h3, creating a slight hierarchy inconsistency though the text matches the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Conclusion and Management Implications' appears as a major section heading both times. HTML: first instance is <h2>, continuation is <h3>.

findings.description: The page headers ('Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023') and page footers ('X | Page') from the PDF are preserved as repeated div elements throughout the HTML. While not incorrect content, they create visual repetition of boilerplate text that could be consolidated.
findings.evidence:
Multiple instances of '
Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023
' and '
X | Page
' throughout the HTML.

findings.description: In Table 1, the 'Emergent', 'Submersed', 'Floating Leaf', and 'Algae' category header rows are rendered as regular data rows rather than as subheadings or merged rows, but all data values are accurately preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Emergent', 'Submersed', etc. as category labels above their respective species. HTML renders them as regular table rows with empty cells for numeric columns, which is acceptable.

findings.description: The figure/chart data for Figures 1, 2, 7, 9, and 11 are represented as raw text data points rather than actual chart images, but the underlying data values are accurate.
findings.evidence: Charts in PDF are visual bar/line graphs. In HTML, they appear as <div class='chart-data'> blocks with text representations of axis labels and values.
